title: adhatoda andromeda
intro: Adhatoda andromeda, also known as Vasa or Adhatoda, is a medicinal plant native to India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Acanthaceae. The leaves of the Adhatoda andromeda plant have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties. The plant's leaves contain compounds like vasicine, which provide several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, that help alleviate respiratory problems and boost the immune system. Adhatoda andromeda is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, to promote respiratory health and well-being.
